title: Built a safety-first AI trader for covered calls and cash-secured puts
author: HelloEarthSpaceWorld
selftext:
I was a software engineer at Google and TikTok in the Bay Area and built an AI options trader called [PutHouse.com](https://puthouse.com/) and wanted to share how my portfolio earned **$6k options income automatically**, while the stocks gained **$42k from ownership**. It repeatedly wins small amounts because it’s safety-first before maximizing returns. It’s also backtested and finetuned since 2012 with a profitable outcome.

The power of this strategy is it does both options income and stock holding and does not replace stock appreciation because you get to reinvest the profits to buy more stocks and compound returns. This doesn’t do the wheel because it’s best to keep the shares for long-term growth without getting them taken away/assigned.

These strategies are simple covered calls and cash-secured puts. What I’ve done is use AI to automate checking things like live market data and calculating the best safety-first option contract with a high chance of profit, then placing the trade.

Some of the things it checks are:

* OI
* IV
* RSI
* DTE
* VIX
* VRP
* Delta
* Bid/ask spread
* Underwater positions
* Account and position size
* News, events, and earnings

What I’ve seen often is someone pretending to be more technical than they actually are which is misleading because they’re showing videos of moving graphs with no real profitable outcome or they leave out the part where you need to integrate with actual market data and order placement function which their posts do not mention.

I’ve abstracted and automated all of that with a click of a button and you don't need a large account to trade. My average user account size is $50k and some of them start with a $2k account and one stock name. But if you can, having more holdings and cash helps because it diversifies income sources. For example, in my portfolio, income came from using **NVDA, TSLA, HOOD, SOFI**, and others using the shares and cash in my account. When one stock is skipped for trading, another one is most likely used.

Depending on market conditions I’ve seen options income up to 3% a month which again is an overlay to stock gains while holding them. I already have users and I improve the product every week based on their feedback. Is there anything you would like answers to or are skeptical about? Happy to make it easier for more people to make money.

comments.author: rpfene
comments.body: Do you automate the ordering processing

replies.author: HelloEarthSpaceWorld
replies.body: Yup, fully automated for the options trading process from contract selection to order placement. You just need to buy the stock you want to hold and earn income with.

comments.author: bly_321
comments.body: Cool! Any plans to support more brokerages?

replies.author: HelloEarthSpaceWorld
replies.body: Not at the moment! Alpaca is one of the few brokerages that are great at API trading and Robinhood doesn't support it yet :/

replies.author: bly_321
replies.body: I use quite a few of the popular ones but Public and Webull are also pretty good.Though I might be an outlier. For context, I’m one of the founders of SnapTrade (a unified broker API) so that’s why I have so many!

replies.author: HelloEarthSpaceWorld
replies.body: Ah I think I've seen your site before. How do you connect with Robinhood with no API? I'd love to connect with their user accounts and place trades but looks like that's not supported with SnapTrade.

replies.author: bly_321
replies.body: They do have an API it’s just that trading isn’t supported on it. Hopefully soon!

replies.author: HelloEarthSpaceWorld
replies.body: Could you point me to it? I only found their Crypto API and an unofficial RH Trading API but not an Account API.

replies.author: bly_321
replies.body: Sorry, there’s no website you can be directed to as the “account API” as you call it is only made available via aggregators (except for that crypto api).

comments.author: blaketran
comments.body: lets say u bought a stock you want to hold and it keeps going down, will puthouse keep the position open and ladder down the calls? or does it do something more advanced

replies.author: HelloEarthSpaceWorld
replies.body: Good question. It doesn’t blindly ladder down calls. The stock position stays yours for long-term ownership unless you choose to sell it, while the short calls are managed by rules like delta thresholds and profit targets. If the call becomes profitable, it closes at 50% profit, then it reassesses the same filters before entering again.
